name: Slow-cooked chicken with a crisp corn crust
intro: wrap, or with a buttery baked potato.

ingredients.item: medium-large red onions, thinly sliced (5 cups/500g)
rose harissa (or 50 percent more or less, depending on variety; see this page)
14 oz/850g boneless, skinless chicken thighs (9 or 10 thighs) salt and black pepper
cup/200g jarred roasted red peppers, drained and cut into ┬Š- inch/2cm thick rounds
tbsp/70g unsalted butter, melted scant 4 cups/500g corn kernels, fresh or frozen and defrosted (from 4 large ears corn)
eggs, yolks and whites separated salt

method: Heat the oil in a large saut├® pan with a lid over medium-high heat. Add the onions and fry for 8ŌĆō9 minutes, stirring a few times, until caramelized and soft. Decrease the heat to medium and add the garlic, harissa, paprika, chicken, 1 tsp salt, and a good grind of black pepper. Cook for 5 minutes longer, stirring frequently, then add the tomato puree and tomatoes. Add the water, bring to a boil, then simmer over medium heat, covered, for 30 minutes, stirring every once in a while.

Add the peppers and chocolate and continue to simmer for 35ŌĆō40 minutes, with the pan now uncovered, stirring frequently, until the sauce thickens and the chicken is falling apart. Remove from the heat and stir in the cilantro. If you are serving the chicken as it is (as a stew without the batter), itŌĆÖs ready to serve (or freeze, once it has come to room temperature) at this stage. If you are making the corn crust, spoon the chicken into a ceramic baking dish
ŌĆöone with high sides that measures about 8 x 12 inches/20 x 30cmŌĆöand set aside.
Preheat the oven to 400┬░F.

To make the batter, pour the butter into a blender with the corn, milk, egg yolks, and ┬Š tsp salt. Blitz for a few seconds, to form a rough paste, then spoon into a large bowl. Place the egg whites in a separate clean bowl and whisk to form ’Ćü rm peaks. Fold these gently into the runny corn mixture until just combined, then pour the mix evenly over the chicken.
Bake for 35 minutes, until the top is golden brown. Keep an eye on it after
25 minutes to make sure the top is not taking on too much color; you might need to cover it with foil for the ’Ćü nal 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and set aside for 10 minutes before serving.
Trout tartare with browned butter and pistachios

Tartare is all about the quality and freshness of the ingredients. The trout needs to be as fresh as can be, the nuts need to be best quality (always taste them to check for freshness), and the salt ’Ćéaked. If you can get hold of them, use slivered pistachiosŌĆötheir long shape is very elegant and their vibrant green looks greatŌĆö but regular shelled pistachios, roughly chopped, are also ’Ćüne. This is as easy to make as it is impressive to serve.
Serves four as a starter
1 shallot, sliced paper-thin (5 tbsp/30g)
2 lemons: zest ’Ćü nely to get 2 tsp, then juice to get 2┬Į tbsp
┬Į tsp granulated sugar flaked sea salt and black pepper
4 trout fillets, skinless, cut into ┬Į-inch/1┬Įcm pieces (12┬Š oz/360g)
1┬Į tbsp unsalted butter
┬Į tsp cumin seeds
2 tbsp slivered pistachios (or roughly chopped pistachios, if you canŌĆÖt get any), lightly toasted
┬╝ cup/5g tarragon leaves, ’Ćü nely chopped

Place the shallot in a small bowl with 2 tbsp of the lemon juice, the sugar, ┬Į tsp ’Ćé aked salt, and a generous grind of pepper. Using your ’Ćü ngers, massage the sugar and salt into the shallot and set aside.

Put the trout in a separate small bowl along with the oil, lemon zest, the remaining 2 tsp of lemon juice, 1┬Į tsp ’Ćé aked salt, and a generous grind of pepper. Stir and set aside for 30 minutesŌĆönot longer, or the ’Ćü sh will overcook.

Before serving, put the butter and cumin seeds into a small pan and place over medium heat. Melt the butter gently for about 5 minutes, swirling the pan from time to time, until the butter begins to foam, turn brown, smell nutty, and is caramelized.

Divide the trout among four dishes and top with the shallot (discarding any liquid). Sprinkle with the pistachios and tarragon and drizzle with the cumin seedŌĆōbrowned butter. Finish with a little sprinkle of ’Ćé aked salt and serve.
Mackerel with pistachio and cardamom salsa
This is a super impressive (but secretly very easy) summer starter. It also works well as a main course. Serve with some herb-’Ćülled rice.
Serves four as a starter or two as a main
8 cardamom pods (or ┬Į teaspoon of ground cardamom) salt
4 mackerel fillets, skin on and pinbones removed (9┬╝ oz/260g)
1┬╝-inch/3cm piece of ginger, peeled (1 oz/25g)
2 tbsp heavy cream
2 tbsp sour cream
┬Š cup/15g cilantro, ’Ćü nely chopped
┬Į cup/10g basil leaves, ’Ćü nely chopped
2┬Į tbsp shelled pistachios, lightly toasted and roughly chopped
2 limes: ’Ćü nely zest 1 to get 1 tsp, then juice to get 2 tsp; cut the second lime into wedges, to serve
1 green chile, seeded and ’Ćü nely chopped
3 tbsp/50ml sunflower oil
Using the ’Ćé at side of a large knife, crush the cardamom pods to release the seeds. Transfer the seeds to a pestle and mortar and crush them ’Ćünely; you
should have about ┬Į tsp. Discard the pods. If starting with ground cardamom you donŌĆÖt need to do this.
Mix a pinch of cardamom (about Ōģø tsp) with a pinch of salt. Rub this on both sides of the ’Ćü sh and set aside until ready to fry.
Finely grate the ginger, then press the pulp through a sieve (with a bowl underneath); you should get 1 tsp of juice. Discard the pulp and set the liquid aside.
Using a fork (or a small whisk, if you have one), whip the cream until stiff.
Switch to using a spatula and fold in the sour cream, ginger juice, and a small pinch of salt, so you have a soft cream. Keep in the fridge until needed.
Combine the cilantro and basil with the pistachios, the remaining Ōģø tsp of cardamom, the lime zest, lime juice, chile, 4 tsp/20ml of the oil, and Ōģø tsp salt. Set aside.

When ready to serve, put the remaining 1 tbsp plus 2 tsp of oil into a large frying pan and place over high heat. Once very hot, add the mackerel ’Ćü llets, skin side down (the skin should sizzle), and fry for 2 minutes. Press the ’Ćü llets with a ’Ćü sh spatula as they cook, to prevent the skin from curling up. Once the skin is crisp and golden brown, turn over and fry for 1 minute, until golden brown.
Transfer the mackerel to four individual plates. Spoon one-quarter of the ginger cream and the pistachio and cardamom salsa next to each ’Ćüllet. Serve hot, with a wedge of lime.
